SCISA Class AA state final at Porter-Gaud School. Newspaper report listed Pee Dee Academy's record as 28-0 and St. Andrew's final record as 19-3.
RecapTitle: ST. ANDREW'S FALLS IN SCISA CLASS AA VOLLEYBALL FINAL
Recap:
CHARLESTON - Pee Dee Academy of Mullins, S.C., completed an undefeated volleyball season Tuesday by beating St. Andrew's 25-19, 25-13, 28-26 in the SCISA Class AA state final at Porter-Gaud School. The Eagles finished 28-0, while St. Andrew's completed a 19-3 season under the direction of first-year coach Mandy Stinson.

Stinson said her team played well in defeat.

"We asked them to go out on the court and give everything,'' she said. "They fought tooth and nail. We couldn't ask for anything more from them."

The Saints won the SCISA Region 2-AA title with a 10-0 mark. They beat Greenwood Christian and Calhoun Academy twice to reach the state championship after making the semifinals last season.
SourceTitle: ST. ANDREW'S FALLS IN SCISA CLASS AA VOLLEYBALL FINAL

Notes: VS. St. Andrew's; AA 1st round @ Sumter Civic Ctr.
This match is at the Bluffton Campus
